description_long: This object is a brown pine cone, featuring a series of overlapping, woody scales that radiate outward from a central axis. The scales are arranged in a spiral pattern, each with a slightly curved, rough-edged surface, and the tips are lighter in color compared to the darker, central core. The pine cone is compact and conical, with a broad base tapering to a narrower top, and the scales are thick and rigid, giving the object a robust, textured appearance. The surface is matte and rough, with visible ridges and natural irregularities typical of conifer cones. There are no visible leaves or bark, only the woody structure of the cone itself. The coloration ranges from medium to dark brown, with some lighter tan highlights on the edges of the scales.
description: A brown, woody pine cone with overlapping, rough-edged scales arranged in a spiral pattern. The surface is textured and matte, with a conical, robust form.
description_view_0: The pine cone is seen from a slightly angled side, showing the layered, overlapping scales and the broad base.
description_view_1: This view presents the pine cone from the base, highlighting the circular arrangement and density of the scales.
description_view_2: A side view emphasizing the spiral pattern and the tapering shape toward the top of the cone.
description_view_3: The pine cone is viewed from the top, displaying the pointed tip and the concentric arrangement of the scales.
